reasoning: The dominant peak at 517.27 cm⁻¹ is very close to the well-known crystalline silicon first-order optical phonon mode (~520 cm⁻¹). The slight downshift from 520 cm⁻¹ could indicate mild strain or laser heating. The peak at 300.80 cm⁻¹ corresponds to the silicon two-phonon (2TA) overtone mode. The peak at 969.37 cm⁻¹ is consistent with the silicon second-order two-phonon (2TO) mode near 960-970 cm⁻¹. However, the peaks at 1579.94 cm⁻¹ and 2670.19 cm⁻¹ are characteristic of the G band and 2D band of graphitic carbon, respectively. The peak at 618.12 cm⁻¹ could be a silicon multi-phonon feature or related to the carbon contamination. This indicates a silicon substrate with graphitic carbon contamination on its surface.
